zh.lifeSummary: 當Peter de St Martin於大約1219年出生在英國多瑟特瓦雷哈姆時，他的父親Jordan de St Martin20歲，母親Joan Fitzwalter de Neville16歲。他於大約1253年與Joan de Neville結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1291年6月24日在英國哈普爾巴辛斯托可享年73歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Peter de St Martin于大约1219年出生在英国多瑟特瓦雷哈姆时，他的父亲Jordan de St Martin20岁，母亲Joan Fitzwalter de Neville16岁。他于大约1253年与Joan de Neville结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1291年6月24日在英国哈普爾巴辛斯托可享年73岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Southern French: variant of   St  .   Romain  .

variants.name: Romain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Altagrace, Aurore, Camille, Dominique, Edele, Georges, Henri, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Jean-Michel, Jeanmarie.
French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and English: variant of   Roman  .

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(Marák): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(Mařák): metronymic from the female personal name Máří, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máří.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (Märtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho ‘male; mule’ (from Latin masculus ‘male, masculine’), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho ‘sledgehammer’ (from Latin marculus ‘hammer’).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Marcinów, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Peter de St Martin was born about 1219, in Wareham, Dorset, England, his father, Jordan de St Martin, was 20 and his mother, Joan Fitzwalter de Neville, was 16. He married Joan de Neville about 1253. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 24 June 1291, in Basingstoke, Hampshire, England, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Peter de St Martin est né environ 1219, à Wareham, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Jordan de St Martin, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Joan Fitzwalter de Neville, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Neville environ 1253. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 24 juin 1291, à Basingstoke, Hampshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 73 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Peter de St Martin um 1219 in Wareham, Dorset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jordan de St Martin 20 und seine Mutter Joan Fitzwalter de Neville 16. Er hat um 1253 Joan de Neville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 24. Juni 1291 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Basingstoke, Hampshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Peter de St Martin è nato il circa 1219 nel Wareham, Dorset, England, il suo padre, Jordan de St Martin, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Joan Fitzwalter de Neville, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Joan de Neville circa 1253. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 24 giugno 1291, nel Basingstoke, Hampshire, England, all'età di 73 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Peter de St Martin(이)가 대략 1219년 영국 도르세트 워함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jordan de St Martin(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Fitzwalter de Neville(은)는 16세였다. 그는 대략 1253년 Joan de Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1291년 6월 24일 영국 함프실르 바징스토크에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Peter de St Martin nasceu aproximadamente 1219, em Wareham, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Jordan de St Martin, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Joan Fitzwalter de Neville, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan de Neville aproximadamente 1253. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de junho de 1291, em Basingstoke, Hampshire, Inglaterra, com 73 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Peter de St Martin родился около 1219 г. в местности варэам, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Jordan de St Martin было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Fitzwalter de Neville, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Neville около 1253 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 24 июня 1291 г. в местности басингсток, хампшир, Англия в возрасте 73 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Peter de St Martin nació aproximadamente 1219, en Wareham, Dorset, Inglaterra, su papá, Jordan de St Martin, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Joan Fitzwalter de Neville, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Joan de Neville aproximadamente 1253. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 24 de junio de 1291, en Basingstoke, Hampshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 73 años.
